assumptions: The valid_out signal is set to 1 when the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results. Otherwise, it is set to 0.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

Operation-specific functional checks were emitted from stable interface patterns even though the broader contract remains conservative.

checks.description: Observe externally visible state progress after a legal operation.
notes: Sequential functional oracle remains event-based unless a future contract explicitly provides fixed latency.
checks.pass_condition: At least one observable output or progress indicator eventually updates in a manner consistent with state progress; no exact-cycle update is required.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan applies one or more legal sequential operations.

checks.description: Reject premature grouped output before a full valid group has been accepted.
notes: Pre-closure quiet check is concrete because the deterministic stimulus drives only a finite prefix of one group.
checks.pass_condition: Before 4 accepted valid samples have been observed, valid_out must remain low and no grouped output value is claimed.
checks.trigger_condition: When fewer than one full grouped-valid accumulation window is driven.

assumptions: Operation-specific functional checks were emitted from stable interface patterns even though the broader contract remains conservative.

checks.description: Observe one grouped accumulation closure and verify the concrete accumulated value relation.
notes: Structured grouped-valid relation is derived from stable valid/data and output-valid/data naming plus finite plan stimulus.
checks.pass_condition: Before 4 accepted valid samples, valid_out remains low; after the completed group, one output event occurs and data_out equals the accumulated sum.
checks.trigger_condition: When the deterministic stimulus drives one complete grouped valid stream.

checks.description: Count only valid-high samples toward grouped accumulation even when gaps occur between them.
notes: Gap cycles are explicitly treated as non-accepting points in the grouped relation.
checks.pass_condition: Only valid-high samples count toward the 4-sample accumulation group; after the full accepted group, valid_out asserts once and data_out equals the accumulated sum of the accepted samples.
checks.trigger_condition: When the deterministic stimulus applies valid-high samples separated by valid-low gaps.

assumptions: Operation-specific functional checks were emitted from stable interface patterns even though the broader contract remains conservative.

checks.description: Clear partial accumulation on reset and require the later full group to produce the only output event.
notes: Structured grouped-valid relation uses reset only as a finite state-clear event.
checks.pass_condition: Before 4 accepted post-reset valid samples, valid_out remains low; after the later full post-reset group, one output event occurs and data_out equals the accumulated sum of that post-reset group.
checks.trigger_condition: When a partial valid stream is interrupted by reset and a later full valid group is driven.

assumptions: Operation-specific functional checks were emitted from stable interface patterns even though the broader contract remains conservative.

checks.description: Observe externally visible state progress after a legal operation.
notes: Sequential functional oracle remains event-based unless a future contract explicitly provides fixed latency.
checks.pass_condition: At least one observable output or progress indicator eventually updates in a manner consistent with state progress; no exact-cycle update is required.

checks.description: Observe two grouped accumulation closures in one finite valid stream.
notes: Repeated-group relation remains event-based and avoids exact inter-group latency assumptions.
checks.pass_condition: Before each completed group of 4 accepted valid samples, valid_out remains low; after each completed group, one output event occurs and data_out matches that group's accumulated sum.
checks.trigger_condition: When two complete valid groups are driven in one deterministic stream.

checks.description: Do not infer business-level completion or acceptance solely from reset activity.
notes: Clock/reset remain anchors and are not emitted as business outputs.
checks.pass_condition: No safety property is violated by treating reset as control-only; later functional claims require separate post-reset evidence.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is asserted or being released.

checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.trigger_condition: When the linked case applies repeated or back-to-back operations.

assumptions: Handshake groups are treated as heuristic anchors, not fully proven protocol specifications.

checks.description: Treat reset assertion/release as a control event and require reset-safe interface behavior.
notes: Reset is used as an anchor only; it is not treated as a business output.
checks.pass_condition: No acceptance, completion, or externally visible functional progress is claimed solely during reset; post-release observation waits for stable, externally visible behavior.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset 'rst_n' is asserted and later released according to the inferred polarity.

notes: Reset oracle focuses on control-safe behavior instead of value-specific output claims.
